_README: Per-client Amazon SP campaign config. Copy to config.<client>-<market>.json (gitignored; only this TEMPLATE is committed). The agent fills campaigns[] from the operator's text brief. Never carry another client's values. Run: python3 tools/amazon-campaign-builder/build_campaigns.py --config <this file> --preflight → --preview → then --config <this file> to build. Output is a bulk-upload FILE only; uploading it in Campaign Manager > Bulk Operations is a separate operator-confirmed action.
brand: <Brand for the output filename, e.g. Acme>
amazon_account: <advertiser account + country, e.g. Acme US (Seller Central)>

naming._note: Campaign name = variable_order joined by delimiter; ad group name drops the shorter form's prefix/suffix tokens (Goal/AdType/EW/Counter/CampCounter/Date). Default preset is EW (this file's variable_order below), the 8-slot Ecom Wizards convention: Goal | Ad Type | Match Type | Trigger Words | Product Identifier | Keyword | Camp Counter | Suffix. Set `preset: "LEGACY"` to use the pre-v2 6-token order instead (Goal | SP | MatchType | ProductName | TargetDescriptor | EW). Extra tokens available: Counter, CampCounter (Halo/Auto only, blank otherwise), Date, Custom1, Custom2, CampaignType.

defaults._note: Per-campaign fields override these. state=paused is the safety default: campaigns only go live when the operator flips them on after upload (or explicitly asks for enabled). start_date empty = today. Placements are whole-number percentages (0 = no Bidding Adjustment row).

campaigns._note: One entry per campaign spec; SKW fans out to one campaign per keyword, transpose_keywords chunks keywords into keywords_per_campaign-sized campaigns. campaign_type: SKW (exact, Rank) | Halo (exact, Profit) | BMM (broad, optional +modifier) | Phrase | Auto (4 auto groups) | PAT (ASIN targeting via target_asins; match_type ASIN_EXACT or ASIN_EXPANDED). goal/match_type/bidding_strategy empty = campaign-type default. campaign_purpose (optional) overrides the naming-convention.md bidding default and EW trigger word for campaigns whose intent cuts across campaign_type: SHIELD (own-brand SKW/BMM/Phrase, down only), SELF_TARGETING (self-PAT, up and down), CATEGORY (up and down); leave empty for the plain per-type default (Rank SKW=Fixed, Auto=up/down, Halo/BMM/Phrase/PAT=down only).
campaigns.product_name: <short product label for the campaign name>
campaigns.target_descriptor: <targeting label for the name, e.g. generic>
asin: <ASIN, optional for sellers, required for vendors>
